puzzles.name: Margaux's Trunk
puzzles.description: An antique trunk with a three-dial combination lock. Each dial shows numbers 0–99. The combination is a date that mattered to Margaux more than any other.
clues: The 1928 playbill is dated October 31, 1928.
Margaux's diary: 'I will lock away what matters most before the final curtain on the 31st.'
Vivian: 'Margaux always used dates she could never forget — she called it her last Halloween.'
hints: The trunk was locked the night of the final performance.
Think about the date of The Crimson Veil's last show.
October is the 10th month. The show was on the 31st. The year was 1928.

puzzles.name: The Script Cipher
puzzles.description: Red-circled letters in the margins of The Crimson Veil script. Click the circled letters in order through Act III's stage directions to reveal the hidden word.

scriptLines.text: LADY ASHFORD enters from stage left, carrying the tray.
She sets down the tray beside the ornate table.
BARON: 'My dear, you look pale. Have some bread.'
She lifts the cup to her lips, examining the engraved rim.
LADY ASHFORD: 'To our last evening together.'
She drinks deeply. A bitter taste crosses her face.

clues: The circled letters follow the order of the stage directions, not the dialogue.
The effects manual describes a 'prop goblet' used in Act III's poisoning scene.
Diego: 'The annotations focus on Act III — the scene where Lady Ashford drinks from the cup.'
hints: Focus only on the red-circled letters in the stage directions.
There are six circled letters in Act III.
It's the vessel that killed Lady Ashford on stage — and killed Margaux in reality.

puzzles.name: The Lighting Board
puzzles.description: The catwalk lighting control panel. Match the Act III lighting cue by setting each channel's slider and switch to the correct position. The effects manual describes the cue sequence.
clues: The effects manual: 'Act III cue: crimson wash, moonlight blue, center spot, crimson wash.'
The annotated script's margin symbols for Act III: ●○★●
Edwin's notebook maps the symbols: ● = crimson/red, ○ = moonlight/blue, ★ = spotlight/white
hints: The effects manual translates poetic stage directions to actual light colors.
Crimson = red. Moonlight = blue. Center spot = white.
The sequence starts and ends with the same color.

puzzles.name: The Evidence Board
puzzles.description: Two intertwined timelines must be connected: the 1928 murder and the modern-day poisoning. Arrange six pieces of evidence in chronological order.
clues: Cecilia's Letters: She ordered poison from a pharmacist in October 1928.
Script Cipher + Mirror Message: Cecilia swapped the prop goblet for a real one.
Diary + Playbill: Margaux drank the poison on stage, October 31, 1928.
Edwin's Notebook: He discovered the proof decades later and devised the Ghost Project.
Fog Controls + Ghost Costume: Edwin staged the hauntings using the theater's effects.
Poisoned Teacup + Ashworth's testimony: Edwin poisoned Ashworth when he was discovered.
hints: Start with the oldest event — who acted first in 1928?
The 1928 timeline has three steps: plan, execute, result.
The modern timeline also has three steps: discover, stage, cover up.

puzzles.name: The Vanity Mirror
puzzles.description: Use the magnifying glass to reveal Margaux's hidden message on the mirror. Drag the glass across the foggy surface to uncover the wax writing beneath.
puzzles.mirrorMessage: C.D. brings the tea. C.D. changed the goblet. C.D. will be the death of me.
clues: The mirror message: 'C.D. brings the tea. C.D. changed the goblet. C.D. will be the death of me.'
The wall photos show Margaux with 'my dear Cecilia.'
The 1928 playbill lists Cecilia Drake as understudy.
hints: Margaux wrote the initials of her killer on her own mirror.
Think about who her understudy was.
The initials stand for a first and last name.

puzzles.name: The Film Frames
puzzles.description: Four frames from the 1928 rehearsal film have been spliced apart. Drag them onto the light table in the correct order to reconstruct what happened on stage that night.

frames.label: The Toast
frames.description: A woman in a gown raises an ornate goblet toward the audience

frames.label: The Drink
frames.description: She tilts the goblet and drinks deeply, eyes closed

frames.label: The Collapse
frames.description: The woman staggers, clutching the table, goblet falling

frames.label: The Understudy
frames.description: A second woman emerges from the wings, stepping into the light

clues: The script's Act III stage directions describe the sequence: toast, drink, collapse.
The playbill notes Cecilia Drake as understudy.
Margaux's diary: 'In the final scene, Lady Ashford raises the goblet and drinks. Then she falls.'
hints: The sequence follows the plot of The Crimson Veil's final scene.
What happens first in the scene? The toast.
After the lead actress falls, who steps forward to take her place?

puzzles.name: Stella's Lockbox
puzzles.description: Stella's lockbox has four rotating symbol dials. Each dial shows theater prop symbols. Match the symbols to the ones etched on the lock plate — the call board has the key.
clues: The call board lists four props with special markings in the margin.
Stella mentioned she's been tracking what's been 'relocated.'
The symbols on the lockbox match the prop inventory markings.
hints: Look at the call board notice — each listed prop has a symbol next to it.
The four symbols are common theater objects.
Mask, spotlight, curtain, rose — in that order.

puzzles.name: Office Wall Safe
puzzles.description: A modern electronic wall safe with a four-digit PIN keypad. Ashworth set it to something related to the theater's most famous event.
clues: Ashworth's book has a chapter on the Monarch bookmarked to the year of the haunting.
The filing cabinet contains a heritage assessment focused on events of 1928.
Ashworth told Nancy he's been researching the theater's history.
hints: Ashworth set the code to something related to the theater's most famous event.
What year did Margaux Fontaine die?
The year appears everywhere in this theater: playbills, memorials, records.

puzzles.name: Hidden Passage Navigation
puzzles.description: The blueprints show a network of hidden passages behind the theater's walls. Trace the correct route from the basement entrance to find where the ghost has been emerging.
clues: The blueprints show passages connecting the basement to several rooms.
The dressing room has a heating vent with warm air rising from below.
Edwin needed access to Margaux's costume — what room has it?
hints: Which room has a direct connection to the basement that you've noticed?
Think about where the warm air and machinery sounds are coming from.
Edwin needed access to Margaux's costume — what room has it?

puzzles.name: Poison Identification
puzzles.description: Analyze the teacup residue using the alchemy book's reference charts. Add reagents in the right order and observe the reactions to identify the poison compound.

steps.instruction: Step 1: Heat the sample. What color does the residue turn?
steps.result: The residue dries to a fine white powder — a metallic compound.

steps.instruction: Step 2: Add acid reagent. What happens?
steps.result: It fizzes and dissolves — the compound is soluble in acid. Margaux's book notes this property for metallic poisons.

steps.instruction: Step 3: Add the sulfide test. What color precipitate forms?
steps.result: An orange-red precipitate forms — matching the alchemy book's entry for antimony sulfide. This is the same compound Margaux underlined.

clues: The alchemy book has a section on antimony compounds heavily annotated by Margaux.
The teacup residue has a metallic, crystalline appearance.
Margaux's diary mentions a 'metallic taste.'
hints: Margaux annotated one specific section of the alchemy book — what compound?
The same poison was used in 1928 and in the modern day.
It's a metallic element — Margaux wrote 'These are my symptoms exactly' next to it.
